verses.2: and Hamath, also, which borders on it,
verses.4: Behold, the Lord will dispossess her,
verses.5: Ashkelon will see it, and fear;
verses.7: I will take away his blood out of his mouth,
verses.8: I will encamp around my house against the army,
verses.10: I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim
verses.12: Turn to the stronghold, you prisoners of hope!
verses.13: For indeed I bend Judah as a bow for me.
verses.14: The LORD will be seen over them.
verses.15: The LORD of Armies will defend them.
verses.16: The LORD their God will save them in that day as the flock of his people;
verses.17: For how great is his goodness,
